cases.query: How has the transition to the Unity Catalog model registry affected your workflow?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a robust migration tool that preserves lineag
As an ML Engineer, I want an approval workflow for model promotions in Unity Cat

pain_points.description: The transition to the Unity Catalog model registry disrupted CI/CD tooling due to changes in model URI format, leading to broken references across notebooks and pipelines.
The migration to Unity Catalog resulted in lost lineage information for model versions, complicating compliance documentation and requiring manual reconstruction of lineage.
The lack of an approval workflow for model promotions in Unity Catalog raises governance concerns, especially for models impacting financial decisions.
The Unity Catalog migration process is cumbersome and lacks adequate tooling to handle edge cases, resulting in significant manual effort post-migration.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a robust migration tool that preserves lineage information during the transition to Unity Catalog, so that I can maintain compliance documentation without manual reconstruction.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of losing critical lineage data during migration, which is essential for compliance.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an approval workflow for model promotions in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure compliance and governance for models that influence financial decisions.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for oversight in model promotions, which is crucial for regulated industries.

cases.summary: The transition to the Unity Catalog model registry has introduced significant challenges, including disruptions to CI/CD workflows and loss of lineage information, which complicate compliance efforts. Additionally, the absence of an approval workflow for model promotions raises governance concerns, highlighting the need for improved migration tools and governance features.

cases.query: Can you describe any performance issues you're facing when using Databricks SQL?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex mo
As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or budget alerts on serverless SQL w
As a Data Analyst, I want to have reliable JDBC connections to Databricks SQL, s
As an Analytics Engineer, I want to have visibility into the performance factors

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models can take 20-30 seconds before any data is processed, slowing down development iteration.
Cost unpredictability with serverless SQL warehouses can lead to unexpectedly high expenses.
JDBC connectivity issues cause random drops in Tableau connections to Databricks SQL, affecting user experience.
Lack of visibility into query performance causes frustration, as users cannot diagnose slow queries effectively.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models, so that I can iterate on my development faster without long wait times.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the significant overhead caused by lengthy query compilation times during development.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or budget alerts on serverless SQL warehouses, so that I can manage and predict expenses more effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of cost unpredictability associated with serverless warehouses.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to have reliable JDBC connections to Databricks SQL, so that I can maintain a seamless experience when using BI tools like Tableau.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the connectivity issues that disrupt the workflow of BI tool users.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to have visibility into the performance factors of my queries, so that I can diagnose and optimize slow queries without needing to read Spark plans.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of not being able to understand why queries are slow and how to improve them.

cases.summary: Users are experiencing significant performance issues with query compilation times and unpredictable costs associated with serverless SQL warehouses. Additionally, JDBC connectivity problems and a lack of visibility into query performance hinder effective analysis and optimization efforts.

cases.query: How do analysts feel about the performance of their queries?

per_story.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language summary of why my query was slow, so
As an Analytics Engineer, I want a visual timeline breakdown of query execution

pain_points.description: Query performance variance is unpredictable, causing analysts to experience significant delays without understanding the reasons.
Analysts lack visibility into the reasons for slow queries and cannot diagnose performance issues effectively.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language summary of why my query was slow, so that I can understand the performance issues and improve my queries.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for analysts to diagnose and optimize their queries without needing to interpret complex Spark query plans.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want a visual timeline breakdown of query execution phases, so that I can quickly identify where time is being spent and address performance bottlenecks.
user_stories.rationale: This story helps engineers and analysts understand query performance in detail, enabling them to make informed optimizations.

cases.summary: Analysts are frustrated by unpredictable query performance, with the same query taking vastly different times to execute. They lack tools to understand the reasons behind slow queries, which hampers their ability to optimize performance effectively.

cases.query: What are the key pain points in cost management for MLflow artifacts?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define artifact retention policies for MLflow exp
As an ML Engineer, I want to view artifact storage consumption and costs by expe

pain_points.description: Artifact storage costs are growing unchecked due to abandoned experiments and lack of retention policies.
MLflow has no native concept of experiment or artifact retention, leading to indefinite accumulation of artifacts.
Bulk deletion of experiments requires API iteration, with no UI support for bulk operations.
Storage costs for MLflow artifacts are not attributed to specific experiments, making cost management difficult.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define artifact retention policies for MLflow experiments, so that I can automatically clean up stale artifacts and manage storage costs effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for automated management of artifact storage to prevent unnecessary costs.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to view artifact storage consumption and costs by experiment in the MLflow UI, so that I can identify and manage high-cost experiments.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the need for visibility into storage costs to facilitate better cost management.

cases.summary: Key pain points in cost management for MLflow artifacts include unchecked storage costs due to abandoned experiments, lack of retention policies, and insufficient visibility into cost attribution. User stories emphasize the need for automated retention policies and improved cost visibility in the MLflow UI.

cases.query: What limitations are users experiencing with serverless compute in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models
As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or receive budget alerts for serverl
As a Data Scientist, I want to use packages that require system-level dependenci

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models is excessively long, causing delays in development iteration.
Cost unpredictability with serverless compute leads to unexpected expenses during job execution.
Serverless compute does not support packages requiring system-level dependencies, limiting functionality.
JDBC connectivity issues with BI tools like Tableau result in dropped connections and limited parameterization.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models in Databricks SQL, so that I can iterate on my development faster and see results without long delays.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the significant overhead users face when working with complex queries, impacting their productivity.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or receive budget alerts for serverless compute jobs, so that I can manage and predict expenses more effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the stress caused by cost unpredictability in serverless compute environments.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to use packages that require system-level dependencies in serverless compute, so that I can leverage all necessary tools without falling back to regular clusters.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the limitation of serverless compute that restricts the use of essential packages, hindering work.

cases.summary: Users are facing significant limitations with serverless compute in Databricks, particularly around query compilation times, cost unpredictability, and restrictions on package dependencies. These issues are impacting productivity and increasing operational costs, necessitating improvements in these areas.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to DLT pipeline performance and monitoring?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform a selective refresh on a single DLT table
As a Data Engineer, I want to receive actionable error messages in the DLT event
As a Data Engineer, I want automated alerts for pipeline health and performance

pain_points.description: Full refresh of DLT pipelines takes hours, blocking updates and slowing down iteration speed.
Error messages in DLT pipelines are often cryptic and lack actionable information, leading to prolonged downtime.
Current DLT monitoring is manual and lacks proactive alerts for pipeline health, resulting in delayed responses to issues.
Watermarking and late data handling in DLT are poorly documented, leading to silent data drops without warnings.
Persisted logs are not available after cluster termination, making it difficult to diagnose failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform a selective refresh on a single DLT table without restarting the entire pipeline, so that I can update specific data without incurring long downtime.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of lengthy full refreshes that block updates and slow down development.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive actionable error messages in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly diagnose and resolve pipeline failures without opening support tickets.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of cryptic error messages that lead to extended downtime.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want automated alerts for pipeline health and performance issues, so that I can proactively address problems before they impact downstream processes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better monitoring and alerts to avoid manual checks and delayed responses.

cases.summary: Key pain points for DLT pipeline performance and monitoring include lengthy full refresh times that hinder updates, cryptic error messages that complicate failure diagnosis, and a lack of proactive monitoring alerts. Addressing these issues through selective refresh capabilities, improved error reporting, and automated health alerts would significantly enhance the user experience for Data Engineers.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have regarding the lineage graph in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all work
As a Platform Admin, I want to run blast-radius analysis from any table or colum

pain_points.description: The lineage graph stops at workspace boundaries, preventing users from tracing data lineage across multiple workspaces.
Column-level lineage is not captured for all operation types, leading to incomplete lineage information.
There is no automated way to perform blast-radius analysis for upstream schema changes, complicating impact assessments.
Data stewards cannot easily answer compliance questions about data flow without manual investigation across multiple workspaces.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all workspaces in the account, so that I can assess the blast radius of upstream schema changes and answer compliance questions about data flow.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of the lineage graph stopping at workspace boundaries, allowing for comprehensive data governance.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to run blast-radius analysis from any table or column node in the lineage graph, so that I can quickly identify all downstream tables, views, and dashboards affected by upstream changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for automated impact assessments, which is currently lacking.

cases.summary: Users have significant pain points related to the lineage graph in Databricks, particularly around its inability to trace lineage across multiple workspaces and the lack of automated impact analysis. These issues hinder data governance and compliance efforts, highlighting the need for enhanced lineage capabilities.

cases.query: How does the privilege model in Unity Catalog affect your team’s workflow?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a more robust migration tool for Unity Catalog that h
As a Platform Admin, I want stronger metadata isolation in Unity Catalog, so tha
As an ML Engineer, I want an approval workflow for model promotions in Unity Cat

pain_points.description: Migration from Hive metastore to Unity Catalog is challenging due to immature tooling and manual re-tagging of tables.
Lack of strong metadata isolation in Unity Catalog leads to potential data leakage between clients.
The approval workflow for model promotions is missing, leading to compliance issues in regulated environments.
Column-level lineage data is incomplete, making it difficult to provide full traceability for compliance.
Managing permissions for a large number of analysts is still manual and cumbersome.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a more robust migration tool for Unity Catalog that handles edge cases, so that I can reduce the manual effort required to migrate our 3,000 tables.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the significant pain point of manual re-tagging and validation during the migration process.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want stronger metadata isolation in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure compliance with strict data isolation requirements for our clients.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the concern of potential metadata leakage between clients, which is critical for maintaining data privacy.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an approval workflow for model promotions in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure all promotions are reviewed and documented for compliance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a formalized approval process to satisfy regulatory requirements.

cases.summary: The transition to Unity Catalog presents significant challenges, particularly in migration and compliance. Users face high severity pain points related to manual processes and insufficient isolation features. There is a clear need for enhanced tooling and workflows to support data governance and operational efficiency.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to output quality evaluation in the Mosaic AI Playground?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an automated evaluation harness for agent workloads in
As a Prompt Engineer, I want to define evaluation rubrics and rate outputs inlin

pain_points.description: Output quality evaluation is manual and lacks statistical tracking, requiring engineers to copy-paste outputs into spreadsheets for subjective rating.
There is no systematic way to evaluate agent quality beyond manual spot-checking outputs, leading to uncertainty about the impact of changes.
Evaluation results are not tracked in MLflow alongside model metrics, resulting in a lack of unified quality view across classical ML models and LLM agents.
Ground truth dataset management is ad hoc, with evaluation sets typically stored as CSV files in notebooks with no versioning or governance.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an automated evaluation harness for agent workloads in Databricks, so that I can systematically measure agent quality and track changes across versions without manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual evaluation and uncertainty about quality changes when modifying agent configurations.

user_stories.story: As a Prompt Engineer, I want to define evaluation rubrics and rate outputs inline in the Mosaic AI Playground, so that I can track output quality statistically without manual spreadsheets.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual output evaluation and the need for statistical tracking of output quality.

cases.summary: Key pain points in the Mosaic AI Playground include the manual nature of output quality evaluation and the lack of systematic tracking for agent quality changes. Users express a need for automated evaluation tools and integrated tracking within MLflow to enhance their workflows and ensure consistent quality assessment.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations Analytics Engineers face with serverless warehouses?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to set per-warehouse budget caps, so that I can
As an Analyst, I want to see an estimated cost of my query before execution, so

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models can take 20-30 seconds, slowing down development iteration.

Query performance is unpredictable, causing analysts to distrust their results and time estimates.
Lack of visibility into individual query costs before execution makes self-governance impossible for analysts.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to set per-warehouse budget caps, so that I can eliminate unexpected cost overruns and manage expenses effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of unpredictable costs associated with serverless warehouses.

user_stories.story: As an Analyst, I want to see an estimated cost of my query before execution, so that I can make informed decisions and avoid running expensive queries.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of not having visibility into query costs, which leads to unintentional overspending.

cases.summary: Analytics Engineers face significant frustrations with serverless warehouses, primarily related to unpredictable costs and query performance. cases.query: What feature requests do users have for improving data governance in Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descri
As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce a required tag set for feature tables in
As a Data Engineer, I want an automated classifier for PII/PHI columns in Unity

pain_points.description: Unity Catalog search does not support searching by column names, descriptions, or tags, leading to duplicate feature creation.
Lack of enforced description or tag taxonomy results in many empty descriptions, making the catalog less useful.
Gaps in column-level lineage tracking hinder compliance and auditing efforts.
Manual tagging of sensitive columns (PHI/PII) is time-consuming and prone to errors due to lack of automation.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descriptions, and tags in Unity Catalog, so that I can find relevant existing features quickly and avoid duplicating feature engineering efforts.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of duplicate feature creation due to inadequate search capabilities.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce a required tag set for feature tables in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure meaningful descriptions are provided and improve the usability of the catalog.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of empty descriptions that make the catalog less useful.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want an automated classifier for PII/PHI columns in Unity Catalog, so that I can efficiently tag sensitive data without manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual tagging, which is time-consuming and error-prone.

cases.summary: Users have highlighted significant pain points related to data governance in Unity Catalog, particularly around search functionality, tagging enforcement, and lineage tracking. Key feature requests include enhanced search capabilities, mandatory tagging, and automation for sensitive data classification to improve compliance and efficiency.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding model promotion documentation?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Mode

pain_points.description: Current model promotion process relies on Slack messages and spreadsheets, which do not provide an auditable record for compliance.
Any user with EXECUTE privilege can promote models to Production without any required review or approval, leading to governance issues.
Audit logs for model promotions do not capture reviewer identity or approval criteria, making it difficult to demonstrate compliance.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Model Registry, so that I can ensure all model promotions are reviewed and documented for compliance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a structured approval process that meets regulatory requirements and provides an auditable record.

cases.summary: Users in regulated industries face significant challenges with the current model promotion process, which lacks necessary approval workflows and audit trails. The reliance on informal communication methods like Slack for approvals does not satisfy compliance requirements, leading to governance risks.

cases.query: How can Databricks improve the alerting system for job monitoring?

pain_points.description: Currently, there is no built-in alerting mechanism for Auto Loader streams to notify if no files have been processed within a specified time frame.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to configure built-in SLA alerting for Auto Loader streams, so that I can receive notifications if no files are processed within a specified time window.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a more reliable and efficient monitoring solution that reduces engineering overhead and prevents data processing delays.

cases.summary: The current lack of built-in alerting for Auto Loader streams presents significant challenges for Data Engineers, leading to potential delays in data processing. Implementing SLA alerting would streamline monitoring efforts and enhance reliability, ultimately improving operational efficiency.

cases.query: What feature requests do Data Engineers have for improving error diagnostics in DLT?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries i
As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after p

pain_points.description: DLT pipeline failures often result in cryptic runtime errors with insufficient information for diagnosis.
DLT executor logs are not persisted after cluster termination, making it difficult to investigate failures.
Error messages for common failure modes are generic and do not provide actionable insights.
The DLT event log lacks original exception details and offending records, complicating root cause analysis.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly diagnose the root cause of pipeline failures without opening a support ticket.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for actionable error diagnostics to reduce downtime and improve self-sufficiency in troubleshooting.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after pipeline termination, so that I can investigate failures without losing critical log information.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of losing logs that are essential for diagnosing issues after a pipeline failure.

cases.summary: Data Engineers are facing significant challenges with error diagnostics in Delta Live Tables (DLT), primarily due to cryptic error messages and the lack of persistent logs. Structured error codes and improved logging capabilities are critical for enabling self-diagnosis and minimizing downtime during pipeline failures.

cases.query: What issues did the attendees highlight about the SCIM synchronization process?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want the SCIM synchronization process to reliably deactiv

pain_points.description: The SCIM sync process fails intermittently, causing deprovisioned users to retain access for 4-6 hours due to internal server errors.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want the SCIM synchronization process to reliably deactivate users within 1 hour of their Okta account deactivation, so that I can ensure compliance and mitigate security risks.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for timely user deactivation to prevent unauthorized access and meet compliance requirements.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have to improve their experience with notebook environment management?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to edit notebooks in real-time with my teammates, so
As a Data Scientist, I want to save and restore my notebook environment state, s

pain_points.description: Users face silent overwrite risks and merge conflicts when collaborating on notebooks due to lack of real-time editing features.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to edit notebooks in real-time with my teammates, so that we can collaborate without losing work or facing merge conflicts.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for improved collaboration and reduces the time spent resolving conflicts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to save and restore my notebook environment state, so that I can resume my work without the hassle of reinstallation and reloading.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly addresses the pain of losing in-memory state due to cluster terminations, improving productivity.

cases.summary: Users are seeking significant improvements in notebook collaboration and environment management. The lack of real-time editing features leads to merge conflicts and productivity loss, while the need for persistent environment snapshots is critical for efficient workflow continuity.

cases.query: Is there a way to log schema changes in Auto Loader without failing?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to pause the stream and log schema change

pain_points.description: Non-additive schema changes cause stream failures with minimal diagnostic information.
There is no proactive alerting for breaking schema changes, leading to delayed responses.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to pause the stream and log schema changes without failing, so that I can handle schema conflicts asynchronously and avoid downtime.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better handling of breaking schema changes to prevent stream failures and improve operational efficiency.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with Auto Loader when non-additive schema changes occur, leading to stream failures and operational downtime. Implementing a feature that allows logging of schema changes without failing the stream would greatly enhance the user experience and reduce the time spent diagnosing issues.

cases.query: What user needs are unmet regarding production LLM application quality?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an evaluation harness for LLM agents that integrates w
As an ML Engineer, I want to manage prompt versions in a unified system alongsid
As an ML Engineer, I want to automate the evaluation scoring process using LLM-a

pain_points.description: There is no systematic way to evaluate LLM agent quality, leading to reliance on manual spot-checking and uncertainty about performance changes.
Prompt versioning is not managed effectively, resulting in deployments using outdated prompts and causing quality regressions.
LLM evaluation processes are time-consuming and require manual scoring, which is inefficient and prone to errors.
There is no built-in mechanism for tracking and managing ground truth datasets, leading to ad hoc practices and potential inconsistencies.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an evaluation harness for LLM agents that integrates with MLflow, so that I can systematically assess agent quality and track improvements over time.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a structured evaluation process that reduces reliance on manual checks and provides clear metrics for performance.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to manage prompt versions in a unified system alongside model versions, so that I can ensure the correct prompt is always deployed with the corresponding model.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of outdated prompts being used in production, which can lead to unexpected quality issues.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to automate the evaluation scoring process using LLM-as-judge, so that I can reduce the time spent on manual evaluations and improve consistency.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the inefficiency of current evaluation methods that require extensive manual effort.

cases.summary: The key unmet user needs regarding production LLM application quality include the lack of systematic evaluation frameworks, ineffective prompt version management, and time-consuming manual evaluation processes. Addressing these pain points through structured user stories can significantly enhance the quality assurance practices for LLM applications in Databricks.

cases.query: What challenges did Amara face when updating cluster policies across multiple workspaces?

cases.summary: Amara faced significant challenges when updating cluster policies across multiple workspaces due to the lack of a centralized management interface. This resulted in time-consuming manual updates and the risk of inconsistent governance across workspaces.

cases.query: What challenges do data professionals encounter with automated classification of PII/PHI?

As a Platform Admin, I want policy templates in Unity Catalog that automatically

cases.query: What frustrations do data scientists face when using the Feature Store for feature discovery?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descri
As a Data Scientist, I want the Feature Store to validate point-in-time joins fo

pain_points.description: Feature discoverability is poor, leading to duplicate feature creation.

cases.query: What overall stability issues does Mei-Ling report with long-running training jobs on Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Senior ML Engineer, I want to receive detailed notifications when a cluster
As a Senior ML Engineer, I want the ability to automatically resume training fro

pain_points.description: Occasional random cluster failures during long-running training jobs without clear explanations.
Cluster startup latency for GPU jobs causing significant overhead before training begins.
Lack of automatic checkpoint-and-resume behavior for jobs interrupted by spot preemption, leading to wasted compute.
MLflow artifact upload failures due to lack of resumable upload support, resulting in lost model checkpoints.

user_stories.story: As a Senior ML Engineer, I want to receive detailed notifications when a cluster fails unexpectedly during long-running training jobs, so that I can understand the cause of the failure and take corrective action.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of having no explanation for cluster terminations, which leads to wasted compute resources.

user_stories.story: As a Senior ML Engineer, I want the ability to automatically resume training from the last checkpoint after a spot instance preemption, so that I can avoid redoing work and save on compute costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of wasted compute due to the lack of checkpoint-aware retry mechanisms.

cases.summary: Mei-Ling reports significant stability issues with long-running training jobs on Databricks, including random cluster failures and lengthy startup times for GPU clusters. Additionally, the lack of automatic checkpointing for interrupted jobs and failures in MLflow artifact uploads contribute to inefficiencies and wasted resources.

cases.query: What feature requests do ML engineers have for better evaluating LLM agents?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an mlflow.evaluate()-compatible API for agent workload

Agent evaluation results are not tracked in MLflow alongside model metrics, leading to a fragmented view of quality.
Regression detection is entirely manual, making it difficult to verify quality changes after agent modifications.
Ground truth dataset management is ad hoc, with evaluation sets typically stored as CSV files in notebooks without versioning.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an mlflow.evaluate()-compatible API for agent workloads, so that I can systematically measure agent quality across multiple dimensions and track changes over time.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of lacking a systematic evaluation method for agent quality.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want built-in LLM-as-judge scorers for answer correctness and groundedness, so that I can evaluate agent outputs without manual scoring.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of needing to set up evaluation patterns manually.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to log evaluation results in MLflow alongside model metrics, so that I can have a unified view of quality across classical ML models and LLM agents.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of fragmented quality tracking.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to automate regression detection in CI/CD pipelines using queryable evaluation results from MLflow, so that I can ensure quality after agent changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual regression detection.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to manage ground truth datasets as Unity Catalog tables with version history, so that I can ensure consistent evaluation across experiments.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of ad hoc dataset management.

cases.summary: ML engineers face significant challenges in evaluating LLM agents due to the lack of systematic evaluation methods and integrated tools within Databricks. Key pain points include manual evaluation processes, fragmented quality tracking, and inadequate dataset management. Proposed user stories focus on creating a robust evaluation framework that enhances the measurement and tracking of agent quality.

cases.query: What improvements does the participant suggest for Databricks?

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the connectivity issues that disrupt the workflow of analysts using BI tools.

cases.summary: Participants highlighted significant pain points related to query performance, cost management, and collaboration features in Databricks. Key suggestions for improvement included reducing query compilation times, enhancing JDBC connectivity, and automating metadata synchronization with Unity Catalog. Overall, there is a strong demand for features that improve productivity and operational visibility.

cases.query: What frustrations does the user have with the Jobs API?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive notifications of breaking changes in the J
As a Data Engineer, I want explicit error messages when required parameters are

pain_points.description: Silent breaking changes in the Jobs API task dependency schema caused production job failures and data correctness issues.
Parameter passing across task types breaks silently, leading to incorrect data being processed without clear error messages.
Inadequate deprecation process for the Jobs API leads to repeated issues with Terraform-managed job configurations breaking unexpectedly.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive notifications of breaking changes in the Jobs API, so that I can proactively update my Terraform configurations and avoid production job failures.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of silent breaking changes that lead to significant operational issues.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want explicit error messages when required parameters are missing in the Jobs API, so that I can quickly identify and fix issues in my data pipelines.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of silent fallbacks to default values that cause incorrect data processing.

cases.summary: Users are frustrated with the Jobs API due to silent breaking changes that disrupt production jobs and inadequate error handling for parameter passing. These issues lead to significant operational challenges and data correctness problems, highlighting the need for better communication and error management in the API.

As a Data Engineer, I want to trigger a selective refresh of specific tables in

cases.query: What frustrations do Analytics Engineers face when collaborating on SQL queries?

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges with audit log management in Databricks due to the overwhelming volume of entries generated daily and the absence of built-in alerting mechanisms. This leads to a reliance on custom solutions for monitoring unauthorized access, highlighting a critical gap in security capabilities.

As a Data Engineer, I want actionable error diagnostics in DLT pipelines, so tha

cases.query: What frustrations do you have with Auto Loader’s schema evolution?

As a Data Engineer, I want to configure Auto Loader to pause on breaking schema

There is no proactive alerting for breaking schema changes, leading to delayed responses and extended downtime.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to alert me of breaking schema changes and provide details on what changed, so that I can resolve issues before the stream fails.

user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the need for a more graceful handling of schema changes to minimize downtime and improve operational efficiency.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with Auto Loader's handling of schema evolution, particularly with non-additive changes causing stream failures and the lack of proactive alerting. The accumulation of checkpoint files further complicates management and incurs costs. cases.query: What operational challenges does the team face with Databricks management?

As a Platform Admin, I want a centralized admin interface for managing multiple

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to have better cost visibility and attribution by team and project, so that I can justify our Databricks spending to finance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of poor cost visibility that complicates financial accountability.

cases.summary: The team faces significant operational challenges with Databricks management, including coarse privilege models in Unity Catalog, overwhelming audit log volumes without alerting, and high operational overhead from managing multiple workspaces. cases.query: What are the key pain points related to feature creation in Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descri

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want duplicate detection on feature creation, so that I am warned if I attempt to create a feature that already exists.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly tackles the problem of redundant feature engineering.

cases.summary: Key pain points in Unity Catalog feature creation include inadequate search capabilities that lead to duplicate features, lack of enforced documentation standards, and significant time wasted on rebuilding existing features. Addressing these issues through improved search functionality, enforced tagging, and duplicate detection will enhance feature discoverability and efficiency for data scientists.

cases.query: What unmet needs do Data Engineers have when dealing with Delta Live Tables errors?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries i
As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after p

pain_points.description: DLT pipelines fail with cryptic runtime errors and insufficient diagnostic information, leading to prolonged downtime.
DLT executor logs are not persisted after cluster termination, making it impossible to investigate failures post-mortem.
Error messages for common failure modes are generic and do not provide actionable insights, complicating troubleshooting.

There is no native retry logic for failed DLT pipelines, requiring manual intervention to re-trigger jobs.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly diagnose failures without opening a support ticket.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after pipeline termination, so that I can investigate failures even after the cluster has stopped.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for access to logs for post-mortem analysis of pipeline failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want detailed error messages that specify the root cause of DLT failures, so that I can resolve issues without extensive troubleshooting.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for clarity in error reporting to facilitate faster resolution of issues.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with Delta Live Tables due to cryptic error messages, lack of actionable diagnostics, and insufficient log retention. These pain points lead to extended downtime and reliance on support for troubleshooting. Addressing these issues through structured error reporting and log persistence is critical for improving operational efficiency.

cases.query: What pain points do users experience with the current SQL editor in Databricks?

cases.summary: Users experience significant pain points with the SQL editor in Databricks, particularly around collaboration and query performance. Additionally, unpredictable costs and connectivity issues with BI tools add to user frustration.

cases.query: What overall feedback did Nina provide about the direction of the Mosaic AI platform?

cases.query: Can you summarize the key pain points around conditional branching implementation?

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better visibility into task execution outcomes, which is crucial for debugging and improving workflows.

cases.summary: The key pain points around conditional branching implementation in Databricks Workflows include the fragility of current workarounds, lack of visibility into task execution conditions, and limitations in passing typed outputs and dynamic parallelism. Addressing these issues through user stories can significantly enhance the reliability and usability of the Workflows feature for Data Engineers.

cases.summary: Users have expressed significant unmet needs regarding MLflow functionality, particularly around experiment comparison, artifact management, and compliance documentation. cases.query: What issues did the team encounter with Auto Loader during the last sprint?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to classify schema changes and alert me o

As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to allow graduated handling of schema evo
As a Data Engineer, I want built-in health alerting for Auto Loader streams, so

pain_points.description: Auto Loader fails streams on breaking schema changes with minimal diagnostic information, causing significant downtime.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the limitation of the current all-or-nothing schema evolution mode that leads to stream failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want built-in health alerting for Auto Loader streams, so that I can be notified of stalls and prevent stale data processing.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for monitoring to ensure data freshness and pipeline reliability.

cases.summary: The team encountered significant issues with Auto Loader, including failures on breaking schema changes, accumulation of checkpoint files, and lack of alerts for stream stalls. These pain points highlight the need for improved schema evolution handling and proactive monitoring to enhance reliability and reduce downtime.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of merge conflicts in notebooks that disrupt collaboration.

User stories highlight the need for improved performance, better cost management tools, and enhanced collaboration features to address these challenges.

cases.summary: The team is facing significant challenges with cross-workspace polling due to the lack of native task dependencies in Databricks Workflows. This results in fragile workarounds that can fail and introduce latency in data processing. Addressing these issues is crucial for maintaining efficient and reliable multi-region data pipelines.

cases.summary: ML Engineers face significant challenges in evaluating their RAG pipelines due to a lack of systematic evaluation methods and poor management of ground truth datasets. These issues lead to inefficiencies and potential performance regressions, highlighting the need for integrated evaluation tools within Databricks.

cases.query: What security concerns do users express about the current setup with Databricks and Vault?

As a SecOps Engineer, I want built-in alerting on audit logs for unauthorized ac

cases.summary: Users express significant security concerns regarding the integration between Databricks and HashiCorp Vault, particularly the lack of real-time updates for secrets. Additionally, the overwhelming volume of audit logs without alerting capabilities makes it difficult to monitor for unauthorized access, while metadata leakage in Unity Catalog raises further security issues.

cases.query: How can the Feature Store improve to prevent data leakage incidents?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a temporal validation parameter on FeatureLookup to ch
As a Data Scientist, I want a leakage report artifact logged to the associated M

pain_points.description: The Feature Store does not validate whether joined feature values predate the event timestamp, leading to data leakage incidents.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a temporal validation parameter on FeatureLookup to check for future-state features, so that I can prevent data leakage incidents during model training.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want a leakage report artifact logged to the associated MLflow run, so that I can review temporal distribution statistics and ensure data integrity.
user_stories.rationale: This story provides a mechanism for tracking and understanding potential data leakage, enhancing model reliability.

cases.summary: The Feature Store currently lacks essential validation mechanisms to prevent data leakage during point-in-time feature lookups, resulting in significant risks for model performance. Implementing temporal validation and logging features can help mitigate these issues and improve overall data integrity in machine learning workflows.

cases.query: What issues have been identified with the churn model and its feature store?

cases.query: What issues did the team encounter during the UC migration related to data lineage?

As a Data Steward, I want to capture column-level lineage for all operation type

Lineage queries in Unity Catalog stop at workspace boundaries, preventing complete lineage tracing across multiple workspaces.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to ensure that all lineage edges are transferred during the Unity Catalog migration, so that I can maintain complete lineage history for compliance and governance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Steward, I want to capture column-level lineage for all operation types in Unity Catalog, so that I can provide complete lineage information for compliance inquiries.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of incomplete lineage data, which hinders the ability to answer compliance questions.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enable cross-workspace lineage stitching in Unity Catalog, so that I can trace data lineage seamlessly across multiple workspaces.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the challenge of lineage queries stopping at workspace boundaries, which limits visibility into data flows.

cases.summary: The team encountered significant issues during the Unity Catalog migration, including loss of lineage history for a portion of tables, gaps in lineage data for certain operations, and limitations in tracing lineage across workspace boundaries. These challenges highlight the need for improved migration tools and enhanced lineage capabilities in Unity Catalog to support compliance and governance requirements.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations users have with the Unity Catalog's lineage tracking?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all work
As a Data Steward, I want automated blast-radius analysis for any column change,

pain_points.description: Lineage tracking does not extend across workspace boundaries, limiting visibility of data flow.
Column-level lineage is not captured for all operation types, leading to incomplete lineage graphs.
There is no automated blast-radius analysis to identify downstream impacts of upstream changes.
Data stewards struggle to answer compliance questions due to manual investigation requirements across multiple workspaces.
Lineage history is lost during migration from the old metastore to Unity Catalog, requiring manual reconstruction.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all workspaces in Unity Catalog, so that I can assess the impact of upstream schema changes and answer compliance questions efficiently.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for comprehensive lineage tracking that spans multiple workspaces, which is currently a significant pain point.

user_stories.story: As a Data Steward, I want automated blast-radius analysis for any column change, so that I can quickly identify all downstream tables and dashboards affected without manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly addresses the frustration of not being able to assess the impact of changes on downstream dependencies.

cases.summary: Users are frustrated with Unity Catalog's lineage tracking due to gaps in cross-workspace visibility, incomplete lineage capture for certain operations, and the lack of automated impact analysis. These issues hinder compliance efforts and require manual investigation, leading to inefficiencies in data governance.

cases.query: How does the lack of column-level lineage impact data governance in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all work
As a Data Steward, I want to run impact analysis from any column node in the lin

pain_points.description: The lineage graph in Unity Catalog stops at workspace boundaries, making it difficult to trace data flow across multiple workspaces.

Data stewards cannot easily answer compliance questions about data flow without manual investigation across multiple workspaces.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all workspaces in the account, so that I can assess the blast radius of upstream schema changes and answer compliance questions about data flow.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of incomplete lineage tracking that hinders effective data governance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Steward, I want to run impact analysis from any column node in the lineage graph, so that I can quickly identify all downstream tables and dashboards affected by changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for automated analysis to streamline compliance and governance tasks.

cases.summary: The lack of column-level lineage in Databricks Unity Catalog significantly impacts data governance by preventing comprehensive tracking of data flow across multiple workspaces. This results in challenges for data stewards in answering compliance questions and assessing the impact of schema changes. Addressing these issues through enhanced lineage capabilities is crucial for effective data management.

cases.query: What feature requests do Analytics Engineers have regarding Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want full-text search across feature column names, d
As an Analytics Engineer, I want enforced tag taxonomy for feature tables in Uni
As an Analytics Engineer, I want duplicate detection on feature creation in Unit

pain_points.description: Unity Catalog search only matches table names, not column names, descriptions, or tags, making feature discovery difficult.
Lack of enforced description or tag taxonomy results in many empty descriptions, rendering the catalog less useful.
Duplicate features are frequently created due to the inability to detect similar existing features during feature creation.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want full-text search across feature column names, descriptions, and tags in Unity Catalog, so that I can find relevant existing features in under 2 minutes before building a new one.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of duplicate feature creation and improves feature discoverability.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want enforced tag taxonomy for feature tables in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure meaningful descriptions and tags are consistently applied.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of empty descriptions and enhances the utility of the feature catalog.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want duplicate detection on feature creation in Unity Catalog, so that I can avoid creating redundant features.
user_stories.rationale: This story helps prevent wasted engineering time and ensures consistency in model training sets.

cases.summary: Analytics Engineers have significant pain points regarding feature discoverability and redundancy in Unity Catalog. The inability to search by column names and the lack of enforced metadata standards lead to inefficiencies and duplicate work. Proposed user stories focus on enhancing search capabilities, enforcing tagging, and implementing duplicate detection to streamline feature management.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding low-traffic model endpoints?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a shared warm pool option for low-traffic model endpoi

pain_points.description: Cold start latency for low-traffic model endpoints can exceed 45 seconds, which fails the SLA of 5 seconds.

Building a custom router for multiple low-traffic models adds infrastructure complexity and maintenance burden.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a shared warm pool option for low-traffic model endpoints, so that I can reduce costs while maintaining SLA compliance without cold starts.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a cost-effective solution to manage low-traffic endpoints while ensuring they meet latency requirements.

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges with cold start latencies on low-traffic model endpoints, which can exceed SLA requirements and incur high costs. The lack of flexible options between always-on and scale-to-zero configurations exacerbates this issue, leading to a demand for a shared warm pool solution to optimize resource usage and performance.

cases.query: What collaboration challenges do analysts face in SQL development?

pain_points.description: Analysts cannot collaborate on SQL queries simultaneously, leading to silent overwrites and data loss.
There is no version history for saved queries, making it impossible to recover lost work after an overwrite.
The SQL editor lacks presence indicators, preventing analysts from knowing if someone else is editing the same query.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to edit shared SQL queries collaboratively without losing work, so that I can avoid silent overwrites and ensure data integrity.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical issue of data loss due to concurrent edits by multiple analysts.

cases.summary: Analysts face significant collaboration challenges in SQL development, primarily due to the inability to work on the same query simultaneously, which leads to silent overwrites and data loss. The lack of version history and presence indicators further exacerbates these issues, highlighting the need for improved collaborative features in the SQL editor.

cases.query: What specific feature requests have been identified to enhance cost transparency for teams using Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databrick
As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce mandatory cost-center tags on all cluster
As a Platform Admin, I want to set monthly budget thresholds for each team tag a

pain_points.description: Inability to attribute costs to specific teams, features, or pipelines, leading to awkward conversations with finance.
Approximately 15% of compute runs untagged, resulting in incomplete cost attribution.
Lack of budget enforcement and alerts for teams, causing unplanned cost overruns.
Manual effort required to build cost attribution dashboards, consuming significant engineering resources.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databricks Account Console, so that I can produce a per-team cost breakdown for any 30-day window in under 5 minutes, with zero custom SQL or data exports required.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of not being able to attribute costs effectively, allowing for better financial reporting and accountability.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce mandatory cost-center tags on all cluster provisioning, so that I can eliminate unattributed spend and ensure accurate cost attribution.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly tackles the issue of untagged compute resources, which complicates cost visibility.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to set monthly budget thresholds for each team tag and receive alerts at 80% and 100% of budget, so that I can manage costs proactively and prevent overruns.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better budget management and cost control, which is currently lacking.

cases.summary: The key findings highlight significant pain points related to cost attribution and budget management within Databricks. Users, particularly Platform Admins, are seeking enhanced visibility into costs associated with specific teams and features, as well as tools to enforce tagging and set budget thresholds. Addressing these needs could greatly improve cost transparency and reduce manual reporting efforts.

cases.query: How do current limitations affect the ability to edit SQL queries in Databricks?

As a SecOps Engineer, I want a unified audit log for all secret accesses in Vaul

user_stories.rationale: This story resolves the issue of fragmented audit logs, enhancing compliance and security oversight.

cases.summary: The key unmet needs regarding Vault secret rotation include the lack of real-time integration with Databricks, leading to outdated secrets and manual processes that consume significant engineering resources. cases.query: What concerns were raised about data leakage incidents in the Feature Store?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the FeatureLookup API to validate timestamp specificat

pain_points.description: Data leakage incidents due to silent incorrect behavior in point-in-time feature lookups, leading to degraded model performance.
The FeatureLookup API does not validate whether joined feature values predate the event timestamp, allowing future-state features to be included without warning.
Post-hoc leakage detection requires custom code, which is not implemented by most teams until after a production incident occurs.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the FeatureLookup API to validate timestamp specifications and warn about potential future-state features, so that I can prevent data leakage before model training.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for proactive detection of temporal data leakage, which is critical for maintaining model accuracy.

cases.summary: Concerns about data leakage incidents in the Feature Store primarily revolve around silent incorrect behavior during point-in-time feature lookups, which can lead to significant drops in model performance. The lack of validation in the FeatureLookup API exacerbates this issue, as it allows future-state features to be included without warning, necessitating custom post-hoc leakage detection that most teams do not implement until after incidents occur.

cases.query: What monitoring gaps exist with Auto Loader that impact data processing?

cases.summary: The key monitoring gaps with Auto Loader include the lack of proactive alerts for breaking schema changes and stalled streams, which can lead to significant downtime and data processing issues. Additionally, the accumulation of checkpoint files without a clear pruning strategy poses a cost concern for users.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an advanced experiment comparison view in MLflow, so that I can analyze multiple experiment runs across various hyperparameters without exporting data to external tools.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of unwieldy comparisons in the MLflow UI, which hinders efficient analysis.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to define retention policies for MLflow experiments, so that I can automatically clean up abandoned experiments and reduce unnecessary storage costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of accumulating abandoned experiments that lead to increased costs.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a built-in API for logging model explainability artifacts in MLflow, so that I can easily meet regulatory requirements without custom coding.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a streamlined process for logging explainability artifacts, which is currently cumbersome.

cases.summary: ML Engineers experience significant frustrations with the MLflow UI, particularly regarding the basic functionality for comparing experiments and the lack of retention policies for abandoned experiments. Additionally, the absence of built-in model explainability integration complicates compliance with regulatory requirements. Addressing these pain points could greatly enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of ML workflows.

cases.query: What are the main challenges related to cost management in Databricks?

cases.summary: The main challenges related to cost management in Databricks include poor visibility into costs by team and project, reliance on manual tagging for cost attribution, unpredictable costs from serverless SQL warehouses, and the absence of native budget enforcement and anomaly detection features. pain_points.description: MLflow has no native concept of experiment or artifact retention, leading to unchecked accumulation of artifacts and high storage costs.

Artifacts from runs that were never promoted to the Model Registry are indistinguishable from relevant artifacts, leading to orphaned storage.
Storage costs for MLflow artifacts are not clearly attributed to experiments, making it difficult to manage expenses.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define retention policies for MLflow experiments, so that I can automatically clean up stale artifacts and control storage costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for managing artifact storage costs and preventing accumulation of unused data.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a bulk management UI for experiments in MLflow, so that I can easily delete or archive multiple experiments without API limitations.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of having to manually iterate through the API for cleanup tasks.

cases.summary: Users are frustrated with the lack of native retention policies in MLflow, leading to unchecked artifact accumulation and high storage costs. The absence of bulk management capabilities further complicates cleanup efforts, resulting in orphaned artifacts and unclear cost attribution. Implementing retention policies and a bulk management UI could significantly alleviate these pain points.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to data validation in the churn model?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to validate timestamp joins during t

pain_points.description: Data leakage from look-ahead bias occurs when future feature values are included in training data due to incorrect timestamp joins.
The FeatureLookup API does not validate whether joined feature values predate the event timestamp, leading to silent incorrect behavior.
Post-hoc leakage detection requires custom code, which most teams do not implement until after a production incident.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to validate timestamp joins during training, so that I can avoid data leakage and ensure model quality before deployment.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for early detection of data leakage to prevent significant model performance degradation.

cases.summary: The key pain points related to data validation in the churn model revolve around data leakage due to incorrect timestamp joins and the lack of validation in the FeatureLookup API. These issues lead to silent failures in model quality, necessitating the implementation of validation checks to prevent future incidents.

cases.query: What feature do Data Engineers want to improve their workflow with DLT?

cases.query: What issues have we faced with Auto Loader schema evolution?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive alerts for breaking schema changes before
As a Data Engineer, I want detailed error messages that specify which column cha
As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to classify schema changes and handle bre

pain_points.description: Non-additive schema changes (type changes, column renames, column deletions) cause stream failures with minimal diagnostic information.
When a stream fails on a schema change, the error message does not specify which column changed or what the old vs. new type is.
There is no proactive alerting for breaking schema changes; engineers only learn about them after the stream has failed.
Auto Loader's schema evolution modes do not provide graduated handling for breaking changes, leading to all-or-nothing failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive alerts for breaking schema changes before the stream fails, so that I can take action to prevent downtime.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for proactive notifications to avoid unplanned stream failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want detailed error messages that specify which column changed and the old vs. new type when a stream fails, so that I can quickly diagnose issues.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of diagnosing failures without clear information.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to classify schema changes and handle breaking changes by pausing the stream and alerting me, so that I can resolve issues without losing data flow.
user_stories.rationale: This story provides a solution to the all-or-nothing failure mode currently experienced.

cases.summary: The key issues with Auto Loader schema evolution include uninformative error messages during stream failures, lack of proactive alerts for breaking changes, and the absence of graduated handling for schema changes. Addressing these pain points through user stories focused on alerts, detailed diagnostics, and improved schema change management can significantly enhance the reliability of data ingestion processes.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a local testing capability for DLT expectations and transformations, so that I can validate changes without incurring cloud compute costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of expensive testing processes that slow down development.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want clear documentation and warnings regarding watermarking and late data handling in DLT, so that I can avoid silent data drops and ensure data integrity.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of undocumented behaviors that lead to data discrepancies.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want actionable error diagnostics in DLT pipelines, so that I can quickly identify and resolve issues without needing to open support tickets.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of unhelpful error messages that prolong downtime.

cases.summary: Teams managing DLT pipelines face significant challenges, including lengthy full refresh times that block downstream processes, inadequate testing capabilities, and poor documentation on critical features like watermarking. Additionally, error handling is often insufficient, leading to delays in diagnosing and resolving issues. Addressing these pain points through targeted user stories can enhance operational efficiency and data integrity.

cases.query: What decisions were made to address the pain points in the last sprint?
